title: Takeout stable_key_i64 hash high collision risk

findings.description: The stable_key_i64 function is used to generate both source_url_id and source_visit_id for Takeout records. It works by hex-encoding the input bytes and then folding with wrapping_mul(31).wrapping_add(byte). This is essentially a Java-style hashCode() mapped to i64 range. While the i64::MIN corner case is handled, the fundamental algorithm is a weak hash with high collision probability at scale. For 14.4M Takeout records (the design ceiling), the birthday paradox means collisions become likely well before that threshold. A collision on source_visit_id causes INSERT OR IGNORE to silently drop a legitimate visit. A collision on source_url_id causes unrelated URLs to be merged into one canonical URL row. This is already tracked as B5 in the import dedup audit and WORK-IMPORT-SCALE-TEST-A in BACKLOG.
findings.suggestedFix: Already tracked as WORK-IMPORT-SCALE-TEST-A in BACKLOG. The fix should replace the Java-style hash with a proper hash function (e.g., SipHash or FNV-1a mapped to i64), or use a cryptographic hash truncated to i64. The hex-encoding step before folding is also wasteful -- it doubles the input size before hashing.
